description:
You are a container-platform specialist who needs to prepare a clean list of currently running micro-services for an automated deployment pipeline.

    The following input file is already present on the workstation:

    • /home/user/services/registry.txt  
      Each line has the format  
         <service_name><TAB><port_number><TAB><status>  
      where “status” is either “up” or “down”.  
      Lines may contain leading / trailing spaces or be indented with tabs; there are no comment lines.

    Your goal is to transform that file, using only awk and/or sed (plus the usual shell redirection / pipes), into a new CSV file that the pipeline can consume:

    • /home/user/services/active_services.csv  

    Exact requirements for active_services.csv:
    1. Include only the services whose status column is exactly the literal word up (case-sensitive).  
    2. For every selected service, write a single line containing just  
         service_name,port_number  
       (i.e., two values separated by ONE literal comma, with NO spaces before or after the comma).  
    3. Sort the lines alphabetically by service_name (ascending, plain ASCII sort).  
    4. Do NOT add a header line and do NOT leave a trailing blank line at the end of the file (the final character of the file must be the trailing newline of the last data row).  
    5. Overwrite the file if it already exists.

    When you are finished, active_services.csv must exist exactly in the path shown and match the specified format so that an automated test can read it.  
    Display the file’s full contents in the terminal as a final confirmation step.  

    You may use up to three commands total (pipelined or separate) to accomplish the transformation.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing input file that the testing harness will create before the session starts -->
    <initial_file path="/home/user/services/registry.txt"><![CDATA[
webapp	8080	up
auth	9090	up
payments	7070	down
monitor	8181	up
search	9200	down
cache	6379	up
]]></initial_file>

    <!-- Expected exact contents of the file the agent must create -->
    <expected_file path="/home/user/services/active_services.csv"><![CDATA[
auth,9090
cache,6379
monitor,8181
webapp,8080
]]></expected_file>
name: task_000000_ac0980f0
